overview: This step creates annotations containing different tokenized forms of text units in a document. It uses ICU-based rules to break text content into classified tokens (words, numbers, URLs, etc.) and attaches them as annotations to the filter events. Both source and target content can be tokenized independently, with locale-aware processing.

tokenizeSource.help: Tokenizes the content of the source text units. At least one of **Tokenize Source** or **Tokenize Targets** should be enabled for the step to produce output.

tokenizeTargets.help: Tokenizes the content of all selected target locales. Useful when you need token-level analysis of translated content, e.g. for quality checks or metrics that compare source and target tokens.

WORD.help: Matches runs of characters that constitute a word in the given language. Word boundaries are determined by Unicode rules and are language-aware.

HYPHENATED_WORD.help: Matches words that include hyphens of various types (e.g. `self-contained`, `well-known`). These would otherwise be split into separate `WORD` tokens at hyphen boundaries.

NUMBER.help: Matches numeric values including those with commas or decimal points (e.g. `1,234.56`, `3.14`).

PUNCTUATION.help: Matches punctuation characters as classified by the Unicode Consortium standards.

WHITESPACE.help: Matches whitespace characters as classified by the Unicode Consortium standards, including spaces, tabs, and other Unicode whitespace.

ABBREVIATION.help: Matches limited types of abbreviations such as `pct` in `3.3pct`, `U.S.`, and `USD`.
notes: The abbreviation detection is primarily designed for English abbreviations and may not cover abbreviation patterns in other languages.

INTERNET.help: Matches Internet addresses including URIs and IP addresses.

EMOJI.help: Matches all emoji characters as defined in the Unicode standard.

notes: Only recognizes the US-style `MM/DD/YYYY` format. Other date formats (e.g. `DD/MM/YYYY`, `YYYY-MM-DD`) will not be matched as DATE tokens.
The wiki documents this as primarily supporting US dollar amounts — coverage of other currency formats may be limited.

TIME.help: Matches time expressions using `:` or `.` as separators, in both 24-hour and 12-hour (with AM/PM) formats.

MARKUP.help: Matches runs that begin with `<` and end with `>`, such as HTML and XML tags. Useful for detecting inline markup that appears within text content.

OTHER_SYMBOL.help: Matches various Unicode symbols including mathematical operators, arrows, and other symbol characters.

KANA.help: Matches Japanese Hiragana and Katakana characters. Useful for CJK text processing where you need to distinguish kana from kanji (ideograms).

IDEOGRAM.help: Matches ideographic characters (CJK unified ideographs) as defined by the Unicode Consortium standards. Covers Chinese characters (hanzi), Japanese kanji, and Korean hanja.

processingNotes: The step takes filter events as input and sends filter events as output — it adds token annotations to existing events rather than transforming the event stream.
If no token types are selected, all available token types are generated.
Token detection is rule-based using Unicode character properties and pattern matching, not statistical NLP.

The wiki lists two additional token types (`COMPANY` and `STOPWORD`) that are not exposed in the current schema — `COMPANY` matches patterns like `AT&T`, `P&G`, `Johnson&Johnson`; `STOPWORD` matches frequent words with no significant meaning.
limitations: DATE token only recognizes `MM/DD/YYYY` format — other date formats are not detected.
ABBREVIATION detection is limited to English abbreviation patterns.
CURRENCY detection may be primarily oriented toward US dollar amounts.
